title: Which Michael Caine Character Are You, Really?
dek: Charmer, crook, butler, or burnt-out professor — your answers reveal which iconic Caine role is basically your on-screen twin.

quiz.intro: No wrong answers here — just answer honestly and let's see which legendary Caine performance matches your soul.

questions.question: Someone crosses you in a deal. What's your actual move?

answers.text: Charm my way through it and worry about the fallout later
Cook up an elaborate, audacious plan to get even in style
Go quiet, go cold, and make them regret it personally
Handle it calmly and loyally — I only raise my voice when it truly counts

questions.question: A younger person needs your guidance. How do you handle it?

answers.text: Recruit them into something bigger and pull off one last great plan together
Protect them fiercely, whatever it costs me
Dedicate myself to raising them right, full stop, no exceptions
Teach them the craft, but keep my own secrets close

questions.question: Be honest — how do you really feel about money?

answers.text: I don't care about money — I care about respect
I'd give every penny away for the people I love
I'll spend whatever it takes to perfect the craft
Spent it all having the time of my life, mate
De-escalate quietly and stay loyal to the people who matter
Outsmart it with careful, patient planning
Defuse the whole thing with a bad joke
Dig in stubbornly until reality finally forces me to change

questions.question: How do you show people you actually care about them?

answers.text: Respect earned through shared craft and mutual obsession
Playful teasing that's secretly all affection
Coldly at first — then with a complete, dramatic change of heart
Reluctant, gruff mentorship that turns into something real

questions.question: At a party, people would describe you as...

answers.text: Loud, cheeky, full of embarrassing dad energy
Standing in the corner grumbling about the whole thing
Witty, but a little too fond of the open bar
The charming rogue who's the life of the party

questions.question: What's your biggest flaw, if you're honest?

answers.text: Greed and isolation, at least for most of my life
Self-loathing dressed up as cynicism
I'm emotionally unavailable and I know it
Overconfidence — I always think I can pull it off

questions.question: Someone offers you one huge, risky opportunity. Do you take it?

answers.text: I'll take it, then immediately regret it over a drink
Only if there's fun — or someone attractive — involved
Absolutely — let's assemble a team and go big
Yes, alone if I have to, if it settles a score

questions.question: How do you want people to remember you?

answers.text: Fondly, as unforgettable — flaws and all
As the guy who pulled off the impossible job
As someone you did not want to cross
As someone who was there when it truly mattered

questions.question: Pick the line that sounds most like something you'd say.

answers.text: "We're going to need a bigger plan."
A quiet, menacing one-liner that ends the conversation
"I will always be there for you. No matter what."
"Are you watching closely?"

results.name: Alfie Elkins
results.subtitle: Alfie (1966)
results.description: You're charming, self-aware, and allergic to real commitment — a magnetic presence who'd rather crack wise to the camera than face the consequences of your own choices. Deep down, though, you know the empty feeling of a life spent avoiding what really matters.

results.name: Charlie Croker
results.subtitle: The Italian Job (1969)
results.description: You're stylish, cocky, and always three steps ahead with a plan nobody else could pull off. Loyalty to your crew matters more than the loot, and you'd rather go out on a cliffhanger than play it safe.

results.name: Jack Carter
results.subtitle: Get Carter (1971)
results.description: You're intense, quiet, and absolutely not someone to mess with once you've decided someone's crossed a line. Underneath the cold exterior is a fierce, almost obsessive loyalty to your own people.

results.name: Alfred Pennyworth
results.subtitle: The Dark Knight (2008)
results.description: You're steady, devoted, and quietly the strongest person in any room — the one who shows up for the people you love without ever needing credit for it. Your calm exterior hides a willingness to sacrifice everything for someone else's mission.

results.name: Cutter
results.subtitle: The Prestige (2006)
results.description: You're methodical, private, and take real pride in mastering your craft, even if it means keeping people at arm's length. You'd rather quietly outthink a problem than make a scene about it.

results.name: Nigel Powers
results.subtitle: Austin Powers in Goldmember (2002)
results.description: You're loud, cheeky, and treat life like one long adventure with a punchline attached. Tough love and terrible jokes are basically your entire parenting — and friendship — strategy.

results.name: Ebenezer Scrooge
results.subtitle: The Muppet Christmas Carol (1992)
results.description: You're stubborn, guarded, and slow to open up — but when you finally do change your mind about something, you go all in. Redemption arcs exist for people exactly like you.

results.name: Frank Bryant
results.subtitle: Educating Rita (1983)
results.description: You're witty, self-deprecating, and a little too comfortable drowning your disappointment in a drink — but you're also capable of real, unexpected growth when the right person walks into your life. Cynical on the outside, secretly hopeful underneath.

sources.title: Educating Rita (1983) | Michael Caine Film Review | CitizenCaine
The 1980s films of Michael Caine: Educating Rita (1983) | Film Stories
